question.label: Wait-until-complete
question.name: When you wait until analysis is complete, what are you trying to avoid?
question.description: Drills into the stated pattern to surface the driver behind delayed stakeholder engagement.

answer: That slows trust because stakeholders feel surprised late.

assessment.note: She names a concrete consequence of the pattern—late stakeholder surprise and reduced trust—which reinforces a mild clarity gap around when to communicate, not a wellbeing signal.

new_queue.label: Surprised late
new_queue.name: Stakeholders feeling surprised late — where does that show up most often?

new_queue.label: Gap awareness
new_queue.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?

new_queue.label: Tradeoff pattern
new_queue.name: What do you usually trade off first when pressure rises?

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, what do you want to do differently in the next two weeks?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: I think my technical output is solid, but my influence is uneven.
I tend to wait until analysis is complete before socializing direction.

I get work done, yet I am not consistently shaping decisions early.
Some feedback has been about being clearer with non-technical partners.
